export_control_resources.name: FR 2025-19846 — BIS Affiliates Rule Suspension (Primary Source)
export_control_resources.description: Primary Federal Register notice (90 FR 47201) suspending the Affiliates Rule expansion for one year. Governs Phase 1 (10 Nov 2025 – 9 Nov 2026) and specifies automatic Phase 2 reimposition on 10 Nov 2026.

export_control_resources.name: BIS Adopts 50 Percent Rule for Export Controls
export_control_resources.description: Analysis of BIS adoption of the OFAC-style 50 Percent Rule, key divergences from OFAC (no aggregation; foreign-only scope), and compliance program implications.

export_control_resources.name: BIS Expands Impact of U.S. Export Controls with 50 Percent Rule
export_control_resources.description: Operational impact of the Affiliates Rule on counterparty screening workflows, beneficial-ownership documentation requirements, and integration with existing OFAC SDN programs.

export_control_resources.name: DOC Suspends Enforcement of the Affiliates Rule for One Year
export_control_resources.description: Enforcement-edge analysis of the suspension alongside bundled China Entity List removals; covers conditionality and US-China trade-framework context.

export_control_resources.name: Trade Compliance Flash: BIS Issues Long-Awaited Affiliates Rule
export_control_resources.description: Covers EAR licence exception eligibility, MEU List vs. Entity List treatment under the 50 Percent Rule, and interim compliance steps during the Phase 1 suspension window.

export_control_resources.name: Anthropic — Fable 5 / Mythos 5 Access Suspension Statement
export_control_resources.description: Party-to-dispute primary statement from Anthropic on the BIS is-informed letter and global model-access suspension (12 Jun 2026). Read alongside competitor-discovery caution (Amazon is an Anthropic investor).
export_control_resources.note: Party to dispute — URL unverified by tool run

export_control_resources.name: The Law of the Anthropic Export Controls
export_control_resources.description: Legal analysis of the statutory basis for applying EAR is-informed letter to a SaaS AI API; examines ECRA §4817, EAR §744.22, deemed-export §§734.13–734.16.

export_control_resources.name: Did the US Government Just Set an AI Export Precedent by Blocking Mythos?
export_control_resources.description: Policy analysis on the doctrinal novelty of treating AI API access as a controlled export under EAR; covers capability-as-SaaS theory and allied-access implications.

export_control_resources.name: Governance Analysis: Fable/Mythos Export Control v1.0
export_control_resources.description: Cloud Security Alliance technical governance analysis of the is-informed letter mechanism, SaaS API export control theory, and enterprise compliance implications.

export_control_resources.name: When the Government Pulls the Plug: Anthropic, Export Controls, and the Future of AI Governance
export_control_resources.description: Compliance practitioner analysis covering is-informed letter mechanics, enterprise model-dependency risk, and supply-chain contractual safeguards.

export_control_resources.name: Chip Security Act — H.R. 3447 / S. 1705 (Bill Text)
export_control_resources.description: Primary bill text for the Chip Security Act. Requires BIS/Commerce to mandate location-verification mechanisms on 3A090/4A090-class chips within 180 days of enactment. Senate companion: S. 1705. Verify stage on Congress.gov before citing.
export_control_resources.note: Stage: committee as of 22 Jun 2026 — URL unverified by tool run

export_control_resources.name: SIA Statement on the Chip Security Act
export_control_resources.description: SIA opposition statement arguing location-verification mandate is 'untested/infeasible' and raises competitiveness concerns. Represents chip manufacturers — weight accordingly as interested party.
export_control_resources.note: Interested party (chip manufacturers) — URL unverified by tool run

export_control_resources.name: The Chip Security Act: Separating Fact from Fiction
export_control_resources.description: Advocacy-aligned analysis of CSA feasibility and policy rationale. Interested party — separate factual claims from position advocacy.
export_control_resources.note: Advocacy source — URL unverified by tool run

export_control_resources.name: Chip Security Act Gains Industry Support Letter
export_control_resources.description: Primary trigger for CSA monitoring (18 Jun 2026). Reports circulation of an industry support letter. Single-source; letter text not independently verified.
export_control_resources.note: Single-source trigger — URL unverified by tool run

upcoming_deadlines.title: BIS Affiliates Rule — Phase 2 Snap-Back Takes Effect
upcoming_deadlines.description: Phase 1 (10 Nov 2025 – 9 Nov 2026): one-year suspension of the BIS Affiliates Rule expansion (EAR §744.21). No EAR authorisation needed for controlled-technology sharing with foreign affiliates ≥50% owned by Entity List / MEU List designees during this window. Phase 2 (from 10 Nov 2026, indefinite): full controls automatically reimposed unless BIS publishes a further Federal Register extension before that date. The suspension does NOT require companies to delay compliance preparation — due-diligence infrastructure must be in place before Phase 2.
upcoming_deadlines.authority: U.S. Bureau of Industry and Security (BIS)
upcoming_deadlines.action_required: By 9 Nov 2026: (1) map all foreign-affiliate ownership chains to the ≥50% threshold for every Entity List and MEU List designee in your supply chain; (2) implement or update Technology Control Plans; (3) ensure EAR licence authorisations are on file for any controlled items shared with in-scope affiliates; (4) monitor Federal Register for any extension notice — none may be assumed.

compliance_note: Export controls are enforced across multiple jurisdictions. Monitor BIS (US), UAE IEC (Emirates), DOJ (enforcement), and international regimes (Wassenaar) for changing restrictions on technology, equipment, and services.

active_enforcement_watch.title: BIS Is-Informed Letter — Anthropic Fable 5 / Mythos 5 Global Suspension
active_enforcement_watch.federal_register_status: unpublished — letter not yet in Federal Register as of 18 Jun 2026
active_enforcement_watch.description: Active case: BIS/Dept of Commerce is-informed letter (12 Jun 2026) requiring export licence for access to Anthropic Fable 5 and Mythos 5 under EAR §744.22 and deemed-export provisions (§§734.13–734.16). Global access suspended same day; negotiations ongoing. Letter unpublished. Monitor Federal Register for: (a) published is-informed letter or BIS notice; (b) interim or final rule on 'covered frontier model' or AI SaaS export controls; (c) any EAR §744.22 amendment expanding the AI diffusion rule.
active_enforcement_watch.auto_update_trigger: Flag any Federal Register notice matching watch_terms above; set status to 'escalated' if a formal rule is proposed.
active_enforcement_watch.action_required: Immediate: (1) Audit enterprise AI contracts for model-dependency risk; add suspension/fallback/portability clauses. (2) Conduct deemed-export analysis for AI API access by foreign nationals. (3) Monitor Federal Register for published rule or letter. (4) Assess whether fallback models face same licence trigger.

active_enforcement_watch.title: Chip Security Act (H.R. 3447 / S. 1705) — Location-Verification Mandate
active_enforcement_watch.stage_note: Passed House Foreign Affairs Committee markup 26 Mar 2026. Floor scheduling unconfirmed as of 22 Jun 2026.

trigger_deadlines.note: Clock starts only on enactment — not yet triggered

monitoring_rules: Flag any stage change on H.R. 3447 / S. 1705 (committee → floor → enacted)
On enactment: start 180-day BIS rulemaking clock; surface alongside Affiliates Rule snap-back (10 Nov 2026)
Watch for BIS ANPRM/IFR defining 'feasible and appropriate' verification techniques
Watch for successor/substantially-similar ECCN expansions (scope-creep vector)
Treat advocacy sources (FDD, CAIS, GeoComply, Multibeam, Fortaegis) as interested parties
De-duplicate against existing export-controls entries; cite source URLs; never fabricate stage
contrarian_flags: Location ≠ usage control: verified physical location does not prevent inference-output diversion via API
Relay-attack vector: chips at approved locations can serve compute to remote unauthorized users
Rule-of-construction enforcement gap: 'no kill switch' means no hardware remedy if verification fails
Pre-enactment gap: retroactive coverage of already-exported H100/A100 installed base not addressed
ECCN scope gap: B200/GB200-class chips may need additional successor designations
Vendor credibility: GeoComply/Multibeam/Fortaegis have commercial interests in CSA passage
active_enforcement_watch.description: The Chip Security Act (119th Congress) would require BIS/Commerce within 180 days of enactment to mandate location-verification mechanisms on export-controlled AI chips (3A090, 4A090, and successor ECCNs). Exporters must also report credible diversion information to BIS. Rule of construction prohibits kill switches and geofencing. Stakeholder split: verification vendors support; chip manufacturers (SIA) oppose. Primary trigger: NBC News 18 Jun 2026 industry support letter. Verify stage on Congress.gov before relying on this entry.
